message:
fix(server/mail_ui): use get_agent_by_id_fresh for render_message + render_file_reservations

The mail_ui render paths for messages and file reservations
were calling queries::get_agent_by_id, which reads from the
session-scoped agent cache.  Under the orphaned-agent
scenarios added in the earlier "tolerate orphaned project
metadata" work, that cache can still hold a pre-deletion copy
of the agent, so the rendered HTML would show an old name
instead of the `[unknown-agent-N]` placeholder the rest of
the pipeline emits.

crates/mcp-agent-mail-server/src/mail_ui.rs

- render_message(): swap
    queries::get_agent_by_id(cx, pool, m.sender_id)
  for
    queries::get_agent_by_id_fresh(cx, pool, m.sender_id)
  so the render path always hits the underlying DB row
  (or its absence) directly, bypassing the cache.
- render_file_reservations(): same swap for the per-row
  agent lookup inside the reservations loop.

The `_fresh` variant is already defined on the queries module
and used by other "I need a guaranteed-current read" call
sites (e.g. the doctor repair code path).  This brings the
mail_ui render pipeline into alignment with that convention.

Behavior-wise:
- Happy path (agent row exists): no visible difference; the
  fresh lookup returns the same row.
- Orphaned agent (row deleted after the last cache refresh):
  the fallback `[unknown-agent-<id>]` placeholder now fires
  correctly instead of serving a stale cached name.

The earlier regression test
render_attachments_preserves_messages_when_agent_rows_are_missing
exercises this exact path and pinned the expected behavior
before this fix landed; the test continues to pass with the
new _fresh call because the cache-bypass ensures the
placeholder renders.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
